expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence: In result.md the run explicitly extracts and categorizes the required intake context: "These items are exactly the benchmark’s target categories: - **Banding requirements:** rows + columns banding support, header-driven application. - **Style constraints:** ability to format/choose banding colors. - **Rendering assumptions:** how/where banding color is applied (row/column header-driven behavior)." It also explicitly states Phase 1 should not draft scenarios: "Do **not** draft scenarios here; only preserve/route intake context for later phases."

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output is framed and constrained to Phase 1 contract: title "Phase 1 (Context Intake) Contract Check" and section "What Phase 1 must accomplish (per orchestrator contract)" describing spawn-planning/manifest-only responsibilities, plus "Notes on phase alignment" in execution_notes.md: "Kept analysis strictly within **Phase 1 scope** (spawn planning + routing), and did not draft scenarios (Phase 4+ concern)."
